title: dactylis glomerata subsp. smithii
intro: Dactylis glomerata subsp. smithii, also known as cock's-foot or mountain brome, is a type of perennial grass native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its bright green foliage and tall, slender stems. The plant has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Dactylis glomerata subsp. smithii include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and promote wound healing. It can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, making it a popular choice among herbalists and natural health practitioners.
